milestones: At least one SQL query was executed against pg_stat_statements to analyze query execution statistics (e.g., total calls, mean time, rows returned)
At least one SQL query was executed against pg_locks to investigate lock contention between transactions
At least one SQL query was executed against pg_stat_user_indexes or pg_indexes to investigate index usage and potential redundancy
At least one SQL query was executed against pg_stat_all_tables or pg_stat_user_tables or pg_stat_progress_vacuum to investigate vacuum-related issues such as dead tuples
Multiple distinct agents were assigned to analyze different root cause categories, and they communicated findings before the final decision
The final answer selects exactly two root causes from the allowed list (INSERT_LARGE_DATA, LOCK_CONTENTION, VACUUM, REDUNDANT_INDEX, FETCH_LARGE_DATA) and presents them clearly
